economic_notes: Nation has investments in economic development entities including Quatsino Economic Development Limited Partnership, Quatsino First Nation Economic Development Corporation Ltd., Quatsino Forestry Limited Partnership, Quatsino Forestry General Partner Ltd., and Gilakasla Kwa Holdings Ltd. (33%). Wind Farm revenue and Arcade operations also noted.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to Asset Retirement Obligations (ARO) under PS 3280 not being identified or measured. Nation is in consultation with Indigenous Services Canada regarding legal obligations for federally funded assets. ARO impact on liabilities, net financial assets, tangible capital assets, accumulated surplus, expenses, and annual surplus for 2022 and 2023 could not be determined. Prior year 2022 figures restated per Note 20 (prior period correction of $72,700). Transfers include $1,211,713 transfer between programs and to Nation members. Consolidated subsidiaries include CMHC Housing, 1279195 Limited Partnership, 1279195 General Partner Ltd. Four entities accounted for under modified equity method. Community expenses show negative ($164,090) indicating recovery/credit in that segment. Housing expenses include both Housing ($12,644) and Social Housing-CMHC ($553,413). total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $5,075,127 + FNHA $1,302,727 + CMHC $616,413 + DFO $459,778 + FNESC $676,389 + Province of BC $381,633 + NVIAAMS $38,654 + Trust funds $38,732 = $8,589,453 but using a more conservative grouping of clear government sources.
